original: Scottish nobles reaffirm their independence by signing the Declaration of Arbroath addressed to Pope John XXII
Italian poet Petrarch first sets eyes on his beloved Laura
Tard-Venus robber bands strike at Brignais, France
Adrian Willaert is installed as a singer for Cardinal Ippolito I d'Este
Mughal prince Khusrav, son of Emperor Jahangir, begins a rebellion against his father, put down a few weeks later with the prince blinded as punishment
Heeren XIX (the directors of the Dutch West India Company) ask "to secure Eylands Curacao"
Construction of the Red Fort completed at Shahjahanabad, Mughal Empire (now Dehli, India)
France & Saxony sign alliance
An earthquake devastates Dubrovnik, then an independent city-state
France declares war on Netherlands
Denmark signs Covenant of Hannover
French explorer Louis-Antoine de Bougainville's expedition arrives in Tahiti, claiming it for France and naming it New Cythera
Rama I succeeds King Taksin of Siam (modern day Thailand), who is overthrown in a coup d'état
British prison guards shoot disorderly American POWs, killing seven and injuring between 31 and 60 in the Dartmoor Massacre
Cornerstone laid for 2nd Mormon temple, Nauvoo, Illinois
Jews of Prussia granted equality
US recognizes Liberal government in Mexico's War of Reform
Joseph Smith III, creates the Reorganized Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints by reorganizing the previous church organized by his father, Joseph Smith, Jr.
Battle of Shiloh, Union defeats Confederacy near Pittsburg Landing, Tennessee
Battle of High Bridge, fought near Farmville, Virginia (Appomattox Campaign), inconclusive result (US Civil War)
Grand Army of the Republic forms in Decatur, Illinois, by Dr. Benjamin F. Stephenson
City of Vancouver, British Columbia incorporated
Declaration of Berlin neutralizes Tonga
French troops under capt Archinard occupy Segu, West-Sudan
Andy Bowen & Jack Burke box 7 hrs 19 mins to no decision (111 rounds)
Mormon Temple in Salt Lake City dedicated

First modern Summer Olympic Games open in Athens, Greece; American athlete James Connolly becomes the first modern Olympic champion when he wins the triple jump (then 2 hops and a jump); later places 3rd in long jump and 2nd in high jump
General railroad strike against "worgwetten" (anti-strike laws)
In Holland, railroad and dock workers go out on strike, but the government passes anti-strike bills, calls out troops, and promptly ends the strike on the 13th April
The Kishinev pogrom in Bessarabia begins, forcing tens of thousands of Jews to later seek refuge in Israel and the Western world.
Germany receives invitation from Sultan of Morocco to an international conference to discuss matters relating to Morocco
1st credit union forms in US
American Radio Relay League (organization for hams) founded
German parliament approves unrestricted submarine warfare
The United States declares war on Germany and enters World War I on the side of the Allies
Bavaria proclaims itself a soviet republic
To force German evacuation of the Ruhr area, the French occupy Frankfurt, Darmstadt, and Hanau
King Oliver and his Creole  band record “Dippermouth Blues” during the second day of sessions at Gennett Records in Richmond, Indiana
The first Prefects Board in Southeast Asia is formed in Victoria Institution, Malaysia
4 planes leave Seattle on 1st successful around-the-world flight
Italy fascists receives 65% of vote of parliament
Völkische Block (Nazis) receives 17.8% of vote in Bavaria
1st film shown on an airplane (British Air)
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Forum, Montreal, Quebec: Montreal Maroons beat Victoria Cougars (WHL), 2-0 for a 3-1 series victory
1st transcontinental glider tow completed
Hostess Twinkies invented by bakery executive James Dewar
1st broadcast of "Little Orphan Annie" on NBC-radio
1st Scottsboro (Alabama) trial begins - 9 blacks accused of rape
418 Lutheran ministers arrested in Germany
H Levitt sinks 499 basketball free throws, misses & sinks 371 more
ANP begins telex service in Amsterdam
Tornado, kills 203 & injuring 1,800 in Gainesville Georgia
Teflon invented by American chemist Roy J. Plunkett at DuPont
Great Britain & Poland sign military pact
US & UK agree on joint control of Canton & Enderbury Is (Pacific)
Beginning of operation Bestrafung - German bombers attack Belgrade (17,000 die)
British major general Michael Gambier-Parry captured in North Africa
German bombardment of Piraeus (munitions ship explodes)
Italian forces holding Addis Ababa surrender to British and Ethiopian forces
British & US armies link up in Africa during WW II
British offensive at Wadi Akarit, South Tunisia
Lou Jansen, leader of illegal Dutch political party (CPN) arrested
Jewish nursery at Izieu-Ain, France, overrun by Nazis
Battle of Okinawa: Giant Japanese battleship Yamato heads to Okinawa with orders to beach herself and be destroyed defending the island
The Holocaust: Nazis begin evacuating prisoners from Buchenwald concentration camp
World War II: Dutch city of Coevorden freed from German occupation by Canadian forces
Montreal Canadiens score 3 goals in 56 sec in playoff game against Det
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Island
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Yemen: failed coup by Abdullah Seif el-Islam
Polish communist Gomulka freed from prison
Greek shipping tycoon Aristotle Onassis buys the Hellenic National Airlines (TAE) and founds Olympic Airlines.
NYC ends trolley car service
USSR performs nuclear test (atmospheric tests)
Egypt & Belgium restore diplomatic relations
Intelsat 1 ("Early Bird") 1st commercial geosynchronous communications satellite launched
Mihir Sen swims Palk Strait between Sri Lanka & India
94.5% of East German voters approve new socialist constitution
Gas and gunpowder explosions at sporting goods store, downtown Richmond, Indiana, kills 41 and injures more than 150
HemisFair 1968 opens in San Antonio, Texas
Egypt drops diplomatic relations with Jordan
The Scarman Tribunal Report, an inquiry into the causes of violence during the summer of 1969 in N Ireland, is published, finding that the Royal Ulster Constabulary had been seriously at fault
America launches the Pioneer 11 (Pioneer G) probe to study Jupiter & Saturn
Dock strike in Gent/Antwerp, Belgium
Federal court rules in favor of the Smothers Brothers in breach of contract lawsuit against CBS-TV, awarding them $776,300 USD
Indian troops invade Sikkim
New York Yankee Ron Blomberg becomes MLB's 1st designated hitter; he walks on five pitches from Boston Red Sox Luis Tiant at Fenway Park
200,000 attend rock concert "California Jam" at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California; line-up includes Earth, Wind & Fire; Black Sabbath; Deep Purple; and Emerson, Lake & Palmer
Yankees 1st home game at Shea Stadium, while Yankee Stadium is renovated; beat Cleveland Indians 6-1
Bundy victim Denise Oliverson disappears from Grand Junction, Colorado
Fastest hat trick by a Wash Cap 3 mins 26 secs (Stan Gilbertson)
Judge rules Beatles 1962 Hamburg album can be released
Kingdome opens, Seattle Mariners 1st game, loses to Angels 7-0
Karnataka beat Uttar Pradesh by inn & 193 to win Ranji Trophy
Post It Notes introduced
Belgian government of Mark Eyskens forms
Yugoslav government sends troops to Kosovo
Columbia returns to Kennedy Space Center from White Sands
Largest crowd ever to see a baseball game in Minnesota 52,279
11th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41C): Challenger 5 launches
1st time 11 people in space
Members of Cameroon's Republican Guard unsuccessfully attempt to overthrow the government headed by Paul Biya.
Bombay beat Delhi by 90 runs to win the Ranji Trophy final
Sudan suspends constitution after coup under general Swarreddahab
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
CBS' fact based "Nobody's Child", story of Marie Balter
Soccer ball juggled non-stop for 14:14 hrs
Los Angeles Dodger's GM Al Campanis appears on TV program ABC News: Nightline saying Blacks may not be equipped to be in baseball management, sparking a racial controversy
African American polar explorer Matthew Henson buried next to Robert Peary in Arlington National Cemetery, in Arlington, Virginia
NJ Devils' 1st playoff game; lose to NY Islanders 4-3 (OT) in 1st round
Former child actor Adam Rich arrested for breaking into a pharmacy
NY-NJ Knights 1st home game (Giants Stadium) lose to Frankfurt 27-17
Subhana, becomes 1st Australian woman to become a Zen teacher
1st game at Camden Field, Baltimore, Maryland - Orioles beat Cleveland Indians 2-0
A general strike is declared by communist groups in Nepal.
British Radio Authority licenses Virgin & TV-AM radio licenses
Microsoft announced Windows 3.1, upgrading Windows 3.0
US Supreme Court rules a Nebraska farmer was entrapped by postal agents into buying mail-order child pornography
First test flight of the Ilyushin IL-96M takes place in Moscow
Florida Marlins lose their first-ever game, 4-2, to the LA Dodgers
First scheduled Indians night game at Jacobs Field is rained out
Palestinian suicide bomber kills 7 Israelis & himself
Rockwell B-1B Lancers break 11 world speed records
American baseball playerAlbert Belleshows off his arm by hitting Sports Illustrated photographer Tony Tomsic in the hand prior to a game
"3 Sisters" closes at Criterion Theater NYC
Progress M-34 Launch (Russia)
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, GC at Desert Mountain: Gil Morgan wins first of 3 Champions Tour majors by 6 strokes from Isao Aoki
Pakistan tests medium-range missiles capable of hitting India

Benny Andersson &Björn Ulvaeus' musical "Mamma Mia!", featuring the songs ofABBA, opens at the Prince Edward Theatre in London's West End; transfers twice to other venues; run interrupted by COVID-19 in March of 2020
Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed via impeachment
New Zealand Sign Language is made an official language of New Zealand
6.3 magnitude earthquake strikes near L'Aquila, Italy, killing at least 253 people
US F-18 Hornet crashes into side of apartment building in Virginia with no fatalities
11 people are killed in an attack on a village in Midlu, Nigeria
22 people are killed and 60 are injured by a suicide bombing at an election campaign tent in Baquba, Iraq
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Dhaka: captain Meg Lanning's 44 from 30 balls leads Australia to 106/4; beat England 105/8 to win by 6 wickets (with 29 balls remaining); 3rd consecutive T20 WC
First baby born with DNA from three parents through mitochondrial transfer in Mexico
France passes legislation making it illegal to pay for sex
Canadian Humboldt Broncos junior hockey team involved in a bus crash outside Tisdale, Saskatchewan that kills 16
US representative Blake Farenthold resigns amidst sexual harassment allegations
First ever "Marsquake" seismic event on planet Mars detected by NASA's InSight lander
Tampa Bay Lightning beats Boston Bruins, 6-3 in the season finale for their 62nd regular season win, tying the NHL record held by Detroit Red Wings (1995-96); 128 points, 4th in NHL history
Cyclone Harold makes landfall in Vanuatu, leaving thousands of houses destroyed and 2 dead
Nadia, a tiger at the Bronx Zoo (New York City), tests positive for COVID-19, 1st known case of human-to-cat transmission
US COVID-19 death toll passes 10,000 in six weeks, with more than 356,000 American infected, New York death toll reaches 4,758
Recording-breaking price of $3.25 million for a comic book as Action Comics #1, that introduced Superman for the first time, sells at auction
Hermits Peak fire, New Mexico's largest wildfire starts in Mora County, as a supposedly controlled burn off by US Forest Service, goes onto displace 100 people, burn 341,000 acres, 62 million trees
Military Tribunal in Ouagadougou convicts former Burkina Faso President Blaise Compaoré in absentia, of the 1987 killing of his predecessor Thomas Sankara
Scientists claim to have found dinosaur remains killed on the actual day a giant asteroid struck earth 66 million years ago beginning their extinction, at Tanis fossil site, North Dakota
Guinness world record for largest afro at 5ft 5in (165cm) set for the third time by Aevin Dugas of Reserve, Louisiana
Battle of Thapsus:Julius Caesardefeats Caecilius Metellus Scipio and Marcus Porcius Cato (Cato the Younger) near Thapsus in modern-day Tunisia
Battle of Pollentia: Roman army underStilichodefeats the Visigoths underAlaric I, forcingAlaricto retreat from Italy
Charlemagneconfirms his father Pepin the Short's grant of land in 754 to the Pope of territories belonging to Ravenna in central Italy
Portuguese Council of the Kingdom meets in Coimbra and declares John, Master of the Order of Aviz, as KingJohn I of Portugal
Turkish forces underSultan Mehmed IIbegin the siege of the Byzantine Empire's capital, Constantinople (now Istanbul), which falls on May 29
King of EnglandCharles IIsigns the Carolina Charter, gifting land in the American Southeast to eight friends who had helped him regain the throne
Thomas Pelham-Hollesbecomes British Minister of Foreign Affairs
British KingGeorge IIdismisses ministerWilliam Pitt the Elder, Secretary of State for the Southern Department
Empress of RussiaCatherine the Greatends the tax on men with beards, enacted by TsarPeter the Greatin 1698
John Jacob Astorincorporates the American Fur Company
Joseph Smithand 5 others officially organize the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Mormon church ) in Fayette, New York
William Wordsworthis appointed British Poet Laureate byQueen Victoria
Battle of Sayler's Creek, fought near Farmville, Virginia (Appomattox Campaign), 1/4 of GeneralRobert E. Lee's army cut off, Union victory (US Civil War)
George Eastmanbegins selling Kodak flexible roll film for the first time
James J. JeffriesKOs Jack Finnegan in 1 for heavyweight boxing title
North Pole almost reached by American explorersRobert Pearyand Matthew Henson. Navigational errors discovered decades later in the travel log likely place the expedition a few miles short.
Mahatma Gandhiorganizes a general strike in India to protest the Rowlatt Acts, which allowed political cases to be tried without juries and internment without trial
"A Connecticut Yankee" film based on novel byMark Twain, directed by David Butler, starringWill Rogersis released
Linus Pauling's seminal chemistry work "The Nature of the Chemical Bond" is published establishing valence bond theory
1st Tony Awards:Arthur Miller, David Wayne & Patricia Neal win for theatrical achievements
John Foster Dullesbecomes advisor to US Secretary of State Acheson with a focus on the Indo-Pacific region
31st Academy Awards: "Gigi",Susan HaywardandDavid Nivenwin
PremierGeorges Pompidouforms new French government
During a debate at Westminster on Northern Ireland,Harold Wilsonof the Labour Party claims that a draft Bill for the imposition of direct rule exists
Roberto ClementeDay, Pittsburgh Pirates retire his #21
19th Eurovision Song Contest:ABBAfor Sweden wins singing "Waterloo" in Brighton, England
Firestone World Tournament of Champions won byEarl Anthony11/16
1st quadrophonic movie track: "Ladies & Gentlementhe Rolling Stones"
Baltimore managerEarl Weaverwins his 1,000th game as a skipper, a 5-3 Orioles victory over the Chicago White Sox
Gordie Howecompletes a record 26th NHL season
Middleweight World Boxing Championship bout:Sugar Ray Leonardupsets Marvelous Marvin Hagler at Caesars Palace in Paradise, Nevada
Argentine soccer starDiego Maradonasuspended for 15 months by Italian League for testing positive for cocaine use
George Harrisonperforms his final full concert at London's Royal Albert Hall, in a benefit for the transcendental mediation based Natural Law political party
Voting begins on choice ofElvis Presleypostage stamp
Chuck Jones found guilty of breaking into beauty queenMarla Maples' home
Liberal Supreme Court Justice and author of Roe v Wade,Harry Blackmunresigns
ActorMarlon Brandomakes anti-Semitic remarks about Hollywood on "Larry KingLive", stating that the "Jews control Hollywood." He later apologizes.
Kurdish leaderJalal Talabanibecomes the Iraqi president; Shiite Arab Ibrahim al-Jaafari is named premier the next day
"Star Trek" film reboot directed byJ.J. Abrams, starring Chris Pine and Zachary Quinto premieres in Austin, Texas
Bayern Munich win their 23rd German Bundesliga title with 6 games to spare
Viktor Orbán's Fidesz is re-elected Prime Minister of Hungary
Protests in Lima, Peru by 30,000 against presidential candidate Keiko Fujimori on 24th anniversary of her fatherAlberto Fujimori's coup
US primary elections: Wisconsin won by DemocratBernie Sandersand RepublicanTed Cruz
Chinese PresidentXi Jinpingarrives in Florida for talks with US PresidentDonald Trump
Exhibition of 1960s iconMary Quantopens at London's V&A Museum
Japanese Prime MinisterShinzō Abeannounces state of emergency in seven prefectures and a nearly $1 trillion stimulus package as COVID-19 cases climb
Jeff Bezos, worth $177 billion, tops Forbes Billionaires list of 2,755 people, including new entryKim Kardashian

Washington Capitals left wingAlex Ovechkinbecomes the greatest scorer in NHL history passing Wayne Gretzky with his 895th career goal in a 4-1 loss to the NY Islanders at UBS Arena in Elmont, New York
Cape Colony, the first European settlement in South Africa, is established by the Dutch East India Company under Jan van Riebeeck
World's first animated cartoon is released, "Humorous Phases of Funny Faces" by J. Stuart Blackton

Plane carrying Rwandan PresidentJuvénal Habyarimanaand Burundian President Cyprien Ntaryamira is shot down by surface-to-air missiles, abruptly ending peace negotiations and sparking the Rwandan Genocide. Those responsible have never been identified.
